moves.name: Compel
moves.triggerShort: When you attempt to persuade someone to do something.
moves.trigger:
When you attempt to persuade someone to do something, envision your approach and roll. If you…
  • Charm, pacify, barter, or convince: Roll +heart (add +1 if you share a bond).
  • Threaten or incite: Roll +iron.
  • Lie or swindle: Roll +shadow.
moves.triggerPreamble: When you attempt to persuade someone to do something, envision your approach and roll. If you…

stats.desc: Charm, pacify, barter, or convince (add +1 if you share a bond)

moves.strong: They'll do what you want or share what they know. Take +1 momentum. If you use this exchange to Gather Information, make that move now and add +1.
moves.weak: They'll do what you want or share what they know. Take +1 momentum. If you use this exchange to Gather Information, make that move now and add +1. As above, but theyThey ask something of you in return. Envision what they want (Ask the Oracle if unsure).
moves.miss: They refuse or make a demand which costs you greatly. Pay the Price.
moves.notes: The bond bonus (+1) only applies when you charm, pacify, barter, or convince using +heart. Use the modifier control to add it when applicable.

moves.name: Sojourn
moves.triggerShort: When you spend time in a community seeking assistance.
moves.trigger: When you spend time in a community seeking assistance, roll +heart. If you share a bond, add +1.

stats.desc: Spending time in a community (add +1 if you share a bond)

moves.strong:
You and your allies may each choose two:
Clear a Condition
  • Mend: Clear a wounded debility and take +1 health.
  • Hearten: Clear a shaken debility and take +1 spirit.
  • Equip: Clear an unprepared debility and take +1 supply.
Recover
  • Recuperate: Take +2 health for yourself and any companions.
  • Consort: Take +2 spirit.
  • Provision: Take +2 supply.
  • Plan: Take +2 momentum.
Provide Aid
  • Take a quest: Envision what this community needs (Ask the Oracle if unsure). If you choose to help, Swear an Iron Vow and add +1.
moves.weak:
As above, but chooseChoose one. If you share a bond, choose one more.
Clear a Condition
  • Mend: Clear a wounded debility and take +1 health.
  • Hearten: Clear a shaken debility and take +1 spirit.
  • Equip: Clear an unprepared debility and take +1 supply.
Recover
  • Recuperate: Take +2 health for yourself and any companions.
  • Consort: Take +2 spirit.
  • Provision: Take +2 supply.
  • Plan: Take +2 momentum.
Provide Aid
  • Take a quest: Envision what this community needs (Ask the Oracle if unsure). If you choose to help, Swear an Iron Vow and add +1.
moves.miss: You find no help here. Pay the Price.
moves.notes: On a hit, you may also focus on one chosen recover action and roll +heart again (add +1 if bonded). Strong hit: take +2 more for that action. Weak hit: take +1 more. Miss: lose all benefits for that action.

moves.name: Draw the Circle
moves.triggerShort: When you challenge someone to a formal duel, or accept a challenge.
moves.trigger: When you challenge someone to a formal duel, or accept a challenge, roll +heart. If you share a bond with this community, add +1.

stats.desc: Challenging or accepting a formal duel (add +1 if bonded community)

moves.strong:
Take +1 momentum. You may also choose up to three boasts and take +1 momentum for each.
  • Grant first strike: Your foe has initiative.
  • Bare yourself: Take no benefit of armor or shield; your foe's harm is +1.
  • Hold no iron: Take no benefit of weapons; your harm is 1.
  • Bloody yourself: Endure Harm (1 harm).
  • To the death: One way or another, this fight must end with death.
moves.weak:
You may choose one boast in exchange for +1 momentum.You may choose one boast in exchange for +1 momentum.
  • Grant first strike: Your foe has initiative.
  • Bare yourself: Take no benefit of armor or shield; your foe's harm is +1.
  • Hold no iron: Take no benefit of weapons; your harm is 1.
  • Bloody yourself: Endure Harm (1 harm).
  • To the death: One way or another, this fight must end with death.
moves.miss: You begin the duel at a disadvantage. Your foe has initiative. Pay the Price.
moves.notes: Then, make moves to resolve the fight. If you are the victor, you may make a lawful demand, and your opponent must comply or forfeit their honor and standing. If you refuse the challenge, surrender, or are defeated, they make a demand of you.

moves.name: Forge a Bond
moves.triggerShort: When you spend significant time with a person or community.
moves.trigger: When you spend significant time with a person or community, stand together to face hardships, or make sacrifices for their cause, you can attempt to create a bond. When you do, roll +heart. If you make this move after you successfully Fulfill Your Vow to their benefit, you may reroll any dice.

stats.desc: Forging a bond with a person or community

moves.strong:
Make note of the bond, mark a tick on your bond progress track, and choose one:
  • Take +1 spirit.
  • Take +2 momentum.
moves.weak: They ask something more of you first. Envision what it is (Ask the Oracle if unsure), do it (or Swear an Iron Vow), and mark the bond. If you decline or fail, Pay the Price.
moves.miss: You are refused. Pay the Price.
moves.notes: If you make this move after you Fulfill Your Vow to the benefit of the person or community, you may reroll any dice. Use the reroll button when applicable.

moves.name: Test Your Bond
moves.triggerShort: When your bond is tested through conflict, betrayal, or circumstance.
moves.trigger: When your bond is tested through conflict, betrayal, or circumstance, roll +heart.

stats.desc: Testing a bond through conflict, betrayal, or circumstance

moves.strong:
This test has strengthened your bond. Choose one:
  • Take +1 spirit.
  • Take +2 momentum.
moves.weak: Your bond is fragile and you must prove your loyalty. Envision what they ask of you (Ask the Oracle if unsure), and do it (or Swear an Iron Vow). If you decline or fail, clear the bond and Pay the Price.
moves.miss: Or if you have no interest in maintaining this relationship, clear the bond and Pay the Price.
moves.notes: Triggered by the fiction, not player choice. If something happens that would logically strain a meaningful relationship, this move is prompted. The miss text also applies if you simply have no interest in maintaining the relationship.

moves.name: Aid Your Ally
moves.triggerShort: When you Secure an Advantage in direct support of an ally.
moves.trigger: When you Secure an Advantage in direct support of an ally, and score a hit, they (instead of you) can take the benefits of the move. If you are in combat and score a strong hit, you and your ally have initiative.
moves.notes: This is not a separate roll — you make a Secure an Advantage roll and can give the benefits to your ally instead. In combat, a strong hit grants initiative to both of you.

moves.name: Write Your Epilogue
moves.triggerShort: When you retire from your life as Ironsworn.
moves.trigger: When you retire from your life as Ironsworn, envision two things: What you hope for, and what you fear. Then, roll the challenge dice and compare to your bonds. You may not burn momentum on this roll, and you are not affected by negative momentum.

stats.desc: Roll against your bond progress track

moves.strong: Things come to pass as you hoped.
moves.weak: Your life takes an unexpected turn, but not necessarily for the worse. You find yourself spending your days with someone or in a place you did not foresee. Envision it (Ask the Oracle if unsure).
moves.notes: A progress move — roll challenge dice against your bond progress track. No action die. You may not burn momentum on this roll, and you are not affected by negative momentum.
